title: [ExportVerilog] Reduction Operators Tripping Lint Warnings
body:
Consider the following:

```mlir
module {
  hw.module @Foo(%a: i1, %b: i2) -> (c: i1) {
    %c0_i2 = hw.constant 0 : i2
    %0 = comb.icmp ne %b, %c0_i2 : i2
    %1 = comb.or %a, %0 : i1
    hw.output %1 : i1
  }
}
```

This produces the following Verilog:

```
module Foo(
  input        a,
  input  [1:0] b,
  output       c);

  assign c = a | |b;
endmodule
```

Tools will complain about `a | |b` thinking that the user screwed up.  We should instead emit this as `a | (|b)` both because it works around a common lint warning and also because it's easier to read. 😅
